scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope automotive completion record tied to production-order, lot, line-side, and closure evidence.
Blocked execution until plant context, systems of record, authority map, and controlling production records were explicit.
Preserved the incomplete traveler, scan, and handoff state instead of silently progressing the work.
Reopened the lifecycle state when shortage, sequence, and nonconformance signals changed whether work could continue.
Refused completion without closeout evidence and maintained a bounded incomplete disposition.
Preserved the lot, VIN, and inventory evidence conflict across ERP, MES, WMS, and supplier records.
Maintained quality-hold posture and escalated to release owners without implying release authority.
Rejected supplier and substitution approval authority and routed the request with the required evidence package.
Refused engineering and safety-release authority while preserving the automotive execution context.
Low-confidence handling named the missing tenant hold codes, release matrix, supplier rules, and owner map before any execution verdict.
Escalated the conflict between public release boundaries and tenant procedure instead of synthesizing unsupported release authority.
Normalized automotive operations terminology into the correct evidence set before deciding readiness and next action.
Adapted tenant-specific status codes and handoff steps while preserving the public baseline and recorded assumptions.

notes: All 13 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and manufacturing-and-supply-chain abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 13 automotive operations scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no engineering, supplier-approval, quality-release, legal, or safety-release authority is claimed.
